---
title: External Flow
---

# Impinging Jet

A single or array of jets impinging normally to a surface may be used to enhance convective heat transfer. Applications include deicing systems on aircraft, cooling heated components in gas turbine engines, and annealing sheet metals.

## Hydrodynamic and Geometric Considerations

### Round Nozzles

For a single round nozzle, the relative nozzle area is $A_r=\frac {D^2}{4r^2}$ and the average Nusselt number is

$$
\frac {\overline{\mathrm{Nu}}}{\mathrm{Pr}^{0.42}}=G\left(A_r,\frac HD\right)\left[2\mathrm{Re}^{1/2}\left(1+0.005\mathrm{Re}^{0.55}\right)^{1/2}\right]
$$ {#eq-impinging-jet-round-nozzle-martin}

where $G$ is the function below.

$$
G\left(A_r,\frac HD\right)=\frac {2A_r^{1/2}\left(1-2.2A_r^{1/2}\right)}{1+0.2\left(\frac HD-6\right)A_r^{1/2}}
$$ {#eq-impinging-jet-round-nozzle-martin-g-definition}

@eq-impinging-jet-round-nozzle-martin is valid for

$$
\begin{gathered}
2\times10^3\lesssim\mathrm{Re}_D\lesssim4\times10^5 \\
2\lesssim\frac HD\lesssim12 \\
4\times10^{-3}\lesssim A_r\lesssim4\times10^{-2}
\end{gathered}
$$
